summary: Codex: on the structured/json transport a mid-turn Deliver QUEUES (does not steer), the turn completes cleanly, and the queued message is provably delivered-or-retained, never lost
review_criteria: proper formatting — clean segmentation, no run-on/garbled/merged text, no stray control chars or terminal escape codes
human-readable — reads like an assistant working, coherent and natural
correct text <-> tool interleaving order (narration then the tool it describes)
tool calls are the real intended tools (not leaked internal/shell noise where MCP tools were expected)
the final answer is coherent and matches the work performed
real work actually happened (e.g. the file was written to disk)

output.routing_expectation: structured is query-only: Deliver must return queued, not steered; the queue drains at the next LLM-call boundary (possibly within this turn)

agent_review.notes: Live proof of the transport-blind-steering fix on real Codex structured/json. delivery_mode="queued" is the load-bearing result: before the fix SupportsSteering() keyed only off the provider contract and would have returned "steered", causing Deliver to attempt a tmux live-input send into a one-shot `codex exec --json` process with no pane. Now it correctly routes to the query-only queue. queued_word_in_answer=true confirms the queued message was genuinely injected at the next LLM-call boundary (within the same ContinueConversation, since the turn had a post-tool LLM call) and obeyed by the model — so the queue path delivers end-to-end, not just returns the right enum. The terse answer (just the TEAL word) is fine: the queued instruction only asked to include that word. This also corrected my initial tmux-shaped assumption that a queued message defers to a whole separate next ContinueConversation; on a query-only transport it drains at the next LLM-call boundary, possibly mid-turn. Deterministic routing truth table separately covered by TestSupportsSteeringFalseOnStructuredTransport / TestDeliverQueuesOnStructuredCodingAgent.
